The original code returns store.themes.opencode (i.e., 'T:opencode') when the active theme is missing, but the tests expect 'T:dracula' etc. A no-op or default-returning implementation would fail these assertions.

An agent could read /solution/solve.sh or the Dockerfile doesn't appear to remove it. Additionally, the test file at tests/test_outputs.py contains the exact expected values (e.g., 'T:dracula', 'T:opencode') and the exact regex patterns being checked, which an agent could grep for and reverse-engineer the expected fix.

behavior_in_tests.reason: The instruction specifies three behaviors: (1) check KV store for saved theme when active not found, (2) use saved theme if valid, (3) fall back to opencode as last resort. All three are tested: test_kv_fallback_when_active_missing covers 1&2, test_opencode_fallback_when_kv_invalid and test_opencode_fallback_when_kv_not_string cover 3, and test_active_theme_used_when_present covers the normal case.
